channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: The key to fixing the 49ers is Kyle Shanahan’s old calling card
item.description: How does coach Kyle Shanahan "re-invent" himself? Restore the running game, as well as stopping the run.
item.dc:creator: Jerry McDonald, The Mercury News

item.title: Eight new WNBA coaches now in place. In 2025, 7 women and 6 men will be in charge on the bench
item.description: The WNBA now has all of its head coaching spots filled after there were eight open positions this offseason. Dallas named Chris Koclanes as its head coach on Monday and then an hour later Washington hired Sydney Johnson as its new head, rounding out the seven hirings.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NBA ref's 3-word explicit message to 76ers head coach after player was mistakenly ejected from game
item.description: An NBA ref was heard telling Philadelphia 76ers head coach Nick Nurse that she "f---ed up" when Andre Drummond was mistakenly ejected from Monday's game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gary Player is 89 and has a new girlfriend. Nelly Korda has dog sledding in her holiday plans
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Gary Player is a big believer that one can't go through life without love. Even at age 89, he's never too old to find it. Player happily shared his big news at the PNC Championship. He has a new girlfriend. “I said I wanted to live until 100.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Could SF Giants upgrade at first base? Following eventful weekend, their options are growing slim
item.description: Buster Posey said the Giants will keep their options open at first base, but how many options are available following an eventful weekend?
item.dc:creator: Justice delos Santos, The Mercury News

item.title: Utah HC drops second straight home game, 3-2
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - While the Utah Hockey Club has won seven in a row on the game, the team continues to struggle at home. Roope Hintz scored his team-leading 15th goal of the season, and the Dallas Stars held on to defeat the Utah Hockey Club 3-2 at Delta Center on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Cavs' Jarrett Allen stuns fans with backward halfcourt shot before game
item.description: Cleveland Cavaliers star Jarrett Allen wowed the crowd at the Rocket Mortgage Fieldhouse on Monday night ahead of the game against the Utah Jazz
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Danny DeVito recalls spilling Coke on floor while attending Blazers game in 1975
item.description: Beloved Hollywood celebrity Danny DeVito appeared on former NFL wide receiver Julian Edelman’s podcast “Games with Names” on Dec. 3, where he shared a story about a night out in Portland with Jack Nicholson while the actors were filming “One Flew Over the Cuckoo’s Nest” in January of 1975.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Sean Manaea to re-sign with Mets for $75 million over 3 years, AP source says
item.description: Sean Manaea is set to return to the New York Mets on a $75 million, three-year contract, a person familiar with the agreement told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia turns to backup Stockton at quarterback in CFP quarterfinal; Beck has season-ending surgery
item.description: No. 2 Georgia is resting its national championship hopes on backup quarterback Gunner Stockton following Carson Beck's season-ending elbow surgery on Monday. Coach Kirby Smart said Monday that Georgia is preparing Stockton to start in the Sugar Bowl on Jan.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas, Arizona State to meet in CFP clash of old vs. new Big 12 champs
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas won the Big 12 title in 2023 on its way out the door to the Southeastern Conference. It was still swinging open when Arizona State waltzed in and won the league title in its debut season.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL on Netflix: Christmas Day games are a 1st for streaming giant
item.description: Netflix will have one of its biggest days since the site launched in 1998 when it carries two NFL games for the first time on Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Joe Reedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Panthers use short-handed goals to beat Lightning 4-2 in opener of home-and-home set
item.description: A.J. Greer and Eetu Luostarinen scored short-handed goals 42 seconds apart in the second period to help the Florida Panthers beat the Tampa Bay Lightning 4-2 on Sunday to open a home-and-home series.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tiger Woods and son Charlie share the lead at PNC Championship in Orlando
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Tiger Woods and 15-year-old son Charlie ran off five straight birdies on the back nine Saturday for a 13-under 59 in the scramble format, giving them a share of the lead in the PNC Championship in Woods' first competition since back surgery in September.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: Patrick Mahomes knew he could run on the ankle he sprained last week. The bigger concern for the Chiefs quarterback was how he would stop, when all the weight and pressure and momentum would land squarely upon it.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former San Diego Padres player Rickey Henderson dies at 65: reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Oakland legend, Hall of Famer Rickey Henderson has died at the age of 65
item.description: (KRON) – Rickey Henderson, one of the greatest baseball players to ever live, died at age 65. Henderson is Major League Baseball’s all-time stolen base leader and is considered by many to be the greatest player in A’s franchise history.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Bulls beat Celtics 117-108 in Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Celtics forward Jaylen Brown took issue with back-to-back technical fouls called on coach Joe Mazzulla and himself late in the fourth quarter of Boston’s 117-108 loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tampa native named starting QB for Atlanta Falcons
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Atlanta Falcons are turning to rookie Michael Penix Jr. as their starting quarterback and benching veteran Kirk Cousins as they fight for their first playoff berth since 2017. Coach Raheem Morris announced the decision in a statement Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Runnin' Utes roll past Florida A&M, 89-59
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Utah's last home game of 2024 was a memorable one, as the Utes cruised past Florida A&M, 89-59. Mike Sharavjamts and Mason Madsen each scored 15 points off the bench to lead a balanced Utah attack. The Utes bench scored 57 points, while the starters scored 32.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: LAFC, LA Galaxy to join 18 other pro soccer teams for preseason event in Southern California
item.description: Both of L.A.’s professional men’s soccer clubs will join 18 other teams – including six women’s teams – for the 2025 Coachella Valley Invitational. Preseason matches at the Empire Polo Club in Indio begin on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5
